title: SIV Fault - Ground Fault Detector (GFD)
description: Ground fault detected in SIV output circuit.
steps: Identify which car (1D or 2D) had the first ground fault.
Check SIV status on AUX page
Check for any other SIV fault on Fault page and History page
Turn off VAC by VACIS or trip all cars’ VAC control CBs and MPCBs.
If no ground fault recurs, move the train with both pantograph raised.
Trip ESCCB1 in both DT cars to prevent SIV redundancy
quickTips: Always be cautious when dealing with electrical cabinets
Prefer hot-swap only for relays marked hot-swap.

title: DIR De-Energized
description: All doors closed and locked but DIR is De-Energized
steps: Identify from which car (1D or 2D) the DIR is showing de-energized
Ensure all door-closed indications are glowing on the N7 panel
Check the CEC for tripping of any door-related CBs
Check SEC-1 for DIR relays not picked when all doors are physically closed (Relay LED should glow when picked)
If a faulty relay is found, swap it with another non-critical and hot-swappable relay in SEC-1
If the fault persists, perform movement with double PDB and take the train to the depot
quickTips: Always carry a multimeter when dealing with electrical equipments
Prefer hot-swap only for relays marked hot-swap.
